title: Robots Are Actually Easier to Build Than Cars: Zhang Wei of Shujiji Power Says the Brain of Humanoid Robots Has Reached GPT-3, the Industry Is at an Exponential Inflection Point
excerpt: When will humanoid robots finally leave the exhibition halls and become tools that solve real-world problems? This is the most frequently asked question at the
content_html:
When will humanoid robots finally leave the exhibition halls and become tools that solve real-world problems? This is the most frequently asked question at the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference. On July 19th, Zhang Wei, founder of Zhujidi Dynamics and a tenured professor at the Southern University of Science and Technology, took the stage and presented a set of quite unconventional judgments: The embodied intelligence represented by humanoid robots is currently on an exponential growth curve, and the entire robot's brain system has reached the stage of GPT-3.

Zhujidi Dynamics was founded in 2022, and Zhang Wei clearly defined the company's positioning: a product-oriented host factory with brain capabilities. In recent years, the company's financing rhythm has clearly outlined capital's recognition of its path. In October 2023, it completed nearly 200 million yuan in angel round and Pre-A financing, with investors including Fengrui Capital, Oasis Capital, Lenovo Venture Capital, and Mingshi Venture Capital; in July 2024, Alibaba led the A-round financing, which was also Alibaba's first investment in the field of embodied intelligence;

In 2025, Zhujidi Dynamics completed a series of 500 million yuan in A-round financing within half a year, receiving support from top institutions such as Alibaba and NIO Capital, and later received strategic investment from JD.com. Both parties plan to collaborate in retail, logistics, and service scenarios; in January 2026, the company completed a 200 million USD B-round financing, introducing UAE Leishi Capital, Oriental Fuhai, and Foundation Capital, with existing shareholders continuing to invest; just this July, Zhujidi Dynamics completed a near-200 million USD Pre-IPO financing, with investors including Leishi Capital, Italian financial consortium, listed company Lantek, IDG Capital, and Hefei Binhu Industrial Development Group. Zhang Wei stated that the company has formed a comprehensive shareholder background covering strategic industry players and domestic and foreign financial investment institutions, further enhancing its industrialization, internationalization, and marketization capabilities.

According to Zhang Wei, many people see that robots are still clumsy and can't do much, so they tend to predict their future in a linear way, but the industry is experiencing an exponential transformation, and it cannot wait for results before responding, otherwise it would be too late. He used an example to illustrate the failure of linear reasoning: Bill Gates spent decades and invested heavily to solve the language interaction problem but never succeeded. If he had been asked when it would be solved before the emergence of large models, he might have said another ten or twenty years, but once the technological paradigm changed, a lot of problems were solved in half a year.

A view that may surprise many is that Zhang Wei believes robots are easy to make, and humanoid robots are also easy to make. They are easier to make than lithography machines, airplanes, and even cars, with about one-tenth the number of parts as cars. So why are airplanes flying every day and cars everywhere, while humanoid robots are mostly still in the exhibition hall? His answer is that it's not lack of people who can make the body, but rather the core capability pointed out by the theme of this conference — AI, especially physical AI derived from data from the physical world. Physical AI's characteristics are that its data does not come from the Internet, but from real-life physical experiences, making it one of the most challenging applications of AI. Its ultimate form, physical AI, is essentially imitating the human brain. Zhang Wei added that the human brain doesn't look very difficult from a conceptual perspective. Its task is only to receive instructions, understand intentions, observe the environment, and calculate how to move, essentially a mapping. The real problem blocking the industry is a lack of data. He believes that the technological paradigm has already relatively converged to pure data-driven, and the rest are more engineering and detail issues. All exploration is essentially about reducing the data cost required to complete a task.

Regarding the brain system, Zhang Wei outlined a three-layer structure, similar to Figure's architecture but with significant differences in details. The top layer is similar to the human prefrontal cortex, a thinking engine driven by large language models, visual language models, and even future world models, belonging to the agentic system, responsible for memory management, instruction collection, information understanding, task planning, and decision-making, only thinking, which he called System 2. The middle layer is the visual motor cortex, which receives upper-level decisions, observes the environment, and makes movement plans. The bottom layer is the cerebellum and motor control system, responsible for completing actions, moving without thinking. Zhang Wei emphasized that the real challenge in the industry is not to perfect a single layer, but to coordinate these three layers in a millisecond-level real-time system, and jointly optimize with hardware. Zhujidi Dynamics refers to this direction as the integration of the big brain and small brain technology, which is also one of the company's most important investment directions.

If we must use the GPT moment as a metaphor, Zhang Wei judges that the entire robot's brain system has reached the early stage equivalent to GPT-3, which is completely different from the judgment of many people who think it is still very early. But he warned that the robot's brain is not a single model, but a system composed of large language models, visual language models, world models, memory management, task planning, and motion control. Zhujidi Dynamics' system is called COSA. The model represents ability, while the brain is a whole system composed of multiple models plus memory management, emotional management, and task planning. He gave an example: if System 2 is driven by an LLM, it's like a disabled person lying in bed who can think but cannot move; if driven by a VLM, it's like a disabled person with eyes who can see the world; if adding a world model, it's like Stephen Hawking who learned physics and could understand and predict the physical world. Embodied intelligence aims to let this smart System 2 guide actions, like helping a disabled person do rehabilitation training, gaining skills through data and practice. Zhang Wei also reminded that it took over two years between GPT-3 and GPT-3.5, while the robot's brain is a system, not determined by a single model, which is his different thinking from many others.

On skill learning, Zhang Wei also put forward a counter-conventional view: Skills don't need to be very general to be considered having a brain. A person who cannot tighten a screw or drive a car is still a normal person with a brain. The generality of skills does not represent the level of intelligence. Skill construction depends on what you want to learn first, then what next, and the corresponding data cost. Different skills have great differences in difficulty. For example, dancing, which does not require real-time interaction with the environment, is relatively simple, while climbing stairs, which requires real-time interaction, is much more difficult. Ollie, Zhujidi Dynamics' robot, climbing stairs is an example of real-time big brain and small brain fusion technology because stairs can be largely completed in a simulation environment; cleaning a table must rely on real machine data. Zhang Wei introduced that the company's COSA system, researched last year and released this year, can receive human commands, plan tasks through System 2, and then schedule navigation positioning and perform specific tasks, such as sending a package, where the robot would first take water to the customer and then deliver the package, generating actions in real time. Recently, COSA completed an important upgrade - a fully autonomous, real-time reasoning long-range task, without remote operation or a remote controller, completing full-body movement and operation in a home environment with a single model and a single skill, and the entire model is purely data-driven without expert rules. He stated that besides Figure, very few companies can complete such a general-purpose long-range mobile operation task, and systems that combine full-body movement and operation are particularly rare.

Regarding commercialization, Zhang Wei summarized the fundamental logic of humanoid robots as a general body plus apps. A single skill like dancing may have limited value, but by continuously adding different skills and applications without changing the robot's configuration, eventually a "absorbing" tipping point will appear, and enough applications will begin to gather around the general body. He believes that the host factory is the most critical in the industrial chain because it connects the upper and lower parts. Zhujidi Dynamics positions itself as a product-oriented host factory with brain capabilities, technically matching Figure comprehensively, and more aggressive in industrialization, with the slogan "serve people, not process," meaning that humanoid robots with two legs should serve people instead of production processes, so they do not prioritize industrial scenarios but are more suitable for reception and public services in human environments. He concluded that the key to commercialization is: the data cost of building a skill must be less than the value created by the skill, only then will the skill be worth doing.

blocks.html_en: When will humanoid robots finally leave the exhibition halls and become tools that solve real-world problems? This is the most frequently asked question at the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference. On July 19th, Zhang Wei, founder of Zhujidi Dynamics and a tenured professor at the Southern University of Science and Technology, took the stage and presented a set of quite unconventional judgments: The embodied intelligence represented by humanoid robots is currently on an exponential growth curve, and the entire robot's brain system has reached the stage of GPT-3.

sentences.en: When will humanoid robots finally leave the exhibition halls and become tools that solve real-world problems?
sentences.zh: 人形机器人什么时候才能最终走出展厅，成为解决现实问题的工具呢？

sentences.en: This is the most frequently asked question at the 2026 World Artificial Intelligence Conference.
sentences.zh: 这是2026世界人工智能大会上最常被问到的问题。

sentences.en: On July 19th, Zhang Wei, founder of Zhujidi Dynamics and a tenured professor at the Southern University of Science and Technology, took the stage and presented a set of quite unconventional judgments: The embodied intelligence represented by humanoid robots is currently on an exponential growth curve, and the entire robot's brain system has reached the stage of GPT-3.
sentences.zh: 7月19日，朱基迪动力创始人、南方科技大学终身教授张伟上台，提出了一组颇为标新立异的判断：以人形机器人为代表的具身智能目前正处于指数​​级增长曲线，整个机器人的大脑系统已经达到了GPT-3的阶段。

blocks.html_en: Zhujidi Dynamics was founded in 2022, and Zhang Wei clearly defined the company's positioning: a product-oriented host factory with brain capabilities. In recent years, the company's financing rhythm has clearly outlined capital's recognition of its path. In October 2023, it completed nearly 200 million yuan in angel round and Pre-A financing, with investors including Fengrui Capital, Oasis Capital, Lenovo Venture Capital, and Mingshi Venture Capital; in July 2024, Alibaba led the A-round financing, which was also Alibaba's first investment in the field of embodied intelligence;

sentences.en: Zhujidi Dynamics was founded in 2022, and Zhang Wei clearly defined the company's positioning: a product-oriented host factory with brain capabilities.
sentences.zh: 珠鸡迪动力成立于2022年，张巍明确了公司的定位：以产品为导向、有大脑能力的主机厂。

sentences.en: In recent years, the company's financing rhythm has clearly outlined capital's recognition of its path.
sentences.zh: 近年来，公司的融资节奏已经清晰勾勒出资本对其路径的认可。

sentences.en: In October 2023, it completed nearly 200 million yuan in angel round and Pre-A financing, with investors including Fengrui Capital, Oasis Capital, Lenovo Venture Capital, and Mingshi Venture Capital; in July 2024, Alibaba led the A-round financing, which was also Alibaba's first investment in the field of embodied intelligence;
sentences.zh: 2023年10月，完成近2亿元天使轮及Pre-A轮融资，投资方包括锋锐资本、绿洲资本、联想创投、明石创投； 2024年7月，阿里巴巴领投A轮融资，这也是阿里巴巴在实体智能领域的首笔投资；

blocks.html_en: In 2025, Zhujidi Dynamics completed a series of 500 million yuan in A-round financing within half a year, receiving support from top institutions such as Alibaba and NIO Capital, and later received strategic investment from JD.com. Both parties plan to collaborate in retail, logistics, and service scenarios; in January 2026, the company completed a 200 million USD B-round financing, introducing UAE Leishi Capital, Oriental Fuhai, and Foundation Capital, with existing shareholders continuing to invest; just this July, Zhujidi Dynamics completed a near-200 million USD Pre-IPO financing, with investors including Leishi Capital, Italian financial consortium, listed company Lantek, IDG Capital, and Hefei Binhu Industrial Development Group. Zhang Wei stated that the company has formed a comprehensive shareholder background covering strategic industry players and domestic and foreign financial investment institutions, further enhancing its industrialization, internationalization, and marketization capabilities.

sentences.en: In 2025, Zhujidi Dynamics completed a series of 500 million yuan in A-round financing within half a year, receiving support from top institutions such as Alibaba and NIO Capital, and later received strategic investment from JD.com.
sentences.zh: 2025年，筑基动力在半年内完成了一系列5亿元A轮融资，获得了阿里巴巴、蔚来资本等顶级机构的支持，随后又获得了京东的战略投资。

sentences.en: Both parties plan to collaborate in retail, logistics, and service scenarios; in January 2026, the company completed a 200 million USD B-round financing, introducing UAE Leishi Capital, Oriental Fuhai, and Foundation Capital, with existing shareholders continuing to invest; just this July, Zhujidi Dynamics completed a near-200 million USD Pre-IPO financing, with investors including Leishi Capital, Italian financial consortium, listed company Lantek, IDG Capital, and Hefei Binhu Industrial Development Group.
sentences.zh: 双方计划在零售、物流、服务场景进行合作； 2026年1月，公司完成2亿美元B轮融资，引入阿联酋雷石资本、东方富海、基业资本，现有股东继续投资；就在今年7月，筑基动力完成近2亿美元Pre-IPO融资，投资方包括雷石资本、意大利财团、上市公司Lantek、IDG资本、合肥滨湖产业发展集团等。

sentences.en: Zhang Wei stated that the company has formed a comprehensive shareholder background covering strategic industry players and domestic and foreign financial investment institutions, further enhancing its industrialization, internationalization, and marketization capabilities.
sentences.zh: 张伟表示，公司已形成覆盖战略行业参与者、境内外金融投资机构的全面股东背景，产业化、国际化、市场化能力进一步增强。

sentences.zh: 一个可能让很多人感到惊讶的观点是，张伟认为机器人很容易制造，人形机器人也很容易制造。

sentences.en: They are easier to make than lithography machines, airplanes, and even cars, with about one-tenth the number of parts as cars.
sentences.zh: 它们比光刻机、飞机甚至汽车更容易制造，零件数量约为汽车的十分之一。

sentences.en: So why are airplanes flying every day and cars everywhere, while humanoid robots are mostly still in the exhibition hall?
sentences.zh: 那么为什么飞机天天在飞，汽车到处都是，而人形机器人却大多还在展厅里呢？

sentences.en: His answer is that it's not lack of people who can make the body, but rather the core capability pointed out by the theme of this conference — AI, especially physical AI derived from data from the physical world.
sentences.zh: 他的回答是，并不是缺少能够制造身体的人，而是缺了本次大会主题所指出的核心能力——人工智能，尤其是源自物理世界数据的物理人工智能。

sentences.en: Physical AI's characteristics are that its data does not come from the Internet, but from real-life physical experiences, making it one of the most challenging applications of AI.
sentences.zh: 物理AI的特点是其数据不是来自互联网，而是来自现实生活中的物理体验，这使其成为AI最具挑战性的应用之一。

sentences.en: Its ultimate form, physical AI, is essentially imitating the human brain.
sentences.zh: 它的最终形式是物理人工智能，本质上是在模仿人脑。

sentences.en: Zhang Wei added that the human brain doesn't look very difficult from a conceptual perspective.
sentences.zh: 张伟补充道，从概念的角度来看，人脑看起来并不是很难。

sentences.en: Its task is only to receive instructions, understand intentions, observe the environment, and calculate how to move, essentially a mapping.
sentences.zh: 它的任务只是接收指令、理解意图、观察环境、计算如何移动，本质上是一个映射。

sentences.en: The real problem blocking the industry is a lack of data.
sentences.zh: 阻碍该行业的真正问题是缺乏数据。

sentences.en: He believes that the technological paradigm has already relatively converged to pure data-driven, and the rest are more engineering and detail issues.
sentences.zh: 他认为技术范式已经相对收敛到纯粹的数据驱动，剩下的更多是工程和细节问题。

sentences.en: All exploration is essentially about reducing the data cost required to complete a task.
sentences.zh: 所有探索本质上都是为了减少完成任务所需的数据成本。

sentences.zh: 最上层类似于人类的前额皮质，是由大语言模型、视觉语言模型甚至未来世界模型驱动的思维引擎，属于代理系统，负责记忆管理、指令收集、信息理解、任务规划和决策，只有思维，他称之为系统2。

sentences.en: The middle layer is the visual motor cortex, which receives upper-level decisions, observes the environment, and makes movement plans.
sentences.zh: 中间层是视觉运动皮层，接收上层决策，观察环境，制定运动计划。

sentences.en: The bottom layer is the cerebellum and motor control system, responsible for completing actions, moving without thinking.
sentences.zh: 最底层是小脑和运动控制系统，负责完成动作，无需思考即可移动。

sentences.en: Zhang Wei emphasized that the real challenge in the industry is not to perfect a single layer, but to coordinate these three layers in a millisecond-level real-time system, and jointly optimize with hardware.
sentences.zh: 张伟强调，业界真正的挑战不是完善单层，而是在毫秒级实时系统中协调这三层，并与硬件共同优化。

sentences.en: Zhujidi Dynamics refers to this direction as the integration of the big brain and small brain technology, which is also one of the company's most important investment directions.
sentences.zh: 珠鸡地动力将这一方向称为大脑和小脑技术的融合，这也是公司最重要的投资方向之一。

blocks.html_en: If we must use the GPT moment as a metaphor, Zhang Wei judges that the entire robot's brain system has reached the early stage equivalent to GPT-3, which is completely different from the judgment of many people who think it is still very early. But he warned that the robot's brain is not a single model, but a system composed of large language models, visual language models, world models, memory management, task planning, and motion control. Zhujidi Dynamics' system is called COSA. The model represents ability, while the brain is a whole system composed of multiple models plus memory management, emotional management, and task planning. He gave an example: if System 2 is driven by an LLM, it's like a disabled person lying in bed who can think but cannot move; if driven by a VLM, it's like a disabled person with eyes who can see the world; if adding a world model, it's like Stephen Hawking who learned physics and could understand and predict the physical world. Embodied intelligence aims to let this smart System 2 guide actions, like helping a disabled person do rehabilitation training, gaining skills through data and practice. Zhang Wei also reminded that it took over two years between GPT-3 and GPT-3.5, while the robot's brain is a system, not determined by a single model, which is his different thinking from many others.

sentences.en: If we must use the GPT moment as a metaphor, Zhang Wei judges that the entire robot's brain system has reached the early stage equivalent to GPT-3, which is completely different from the judgment of many people who think it is still very early.
sentences.zh: 如果一定要用GPT时刻来比喻的话，张伟判断整个机器人的大脑系统已经达到了相当于GPT-3的早期阶段，这和很多人认为还很早期的判断完全不同。

sentences.en: But he warned that the robot's brain is not a single model, but a system composed of large language models, visual language models, world models, memory management, task planning, and motion control.
sentences.zh: 但他警告说，机器人的大脑不是单一的模型，而是由大型语言模型、视觉语言模型、世界模型、内存管理、任务规划和运动控制组成的系统。

sentences.en: Zhujidi Dynamics' system is called COSA.
sentences.zh: 诸暨地动力的系统称为COSA。

sentences.en: The model represents ability, while the brain is a whole system composed of multiple models plus memory management, emotional management, and task planning.
sentences.zh: 模型代表能力，而大脑则是由多种模型加上记忆管理、情绪管理、任务规划等组成的一个整体系统。

sentences.en: He gave an example: if System 2 is driven by an LLM, it's like a disabled person lying in bed who can think but cannot move; if driven by a VLM, it's like a disabled person with eyes who can see the world; if adding a world model, it's like Stephen Hawking who learned physics and could understand and predict the physical world.
sentences.zh: 他举了一个例子：如果系统2是由LLM驱动的，那么就像一个躺在床上的残疾人，可以思考但不能动；如果由VLM驱动，就像一个残疾人有眼睛可以看到世界；如果加上世界模型，就像史蒂芬·霍金学了物理，可以理解和预测物理世界。

sentences.en: Embodied intelligence aims to let this smart System 2 guide actions, like helping a disabled person do rehabilitation training, gaining skills through data and practice.
sentences.zh: 具身智能旨在让这个智能系统2指导行动，比如帮助残疾人进行康复训练，通过数据和实践获得技能。

sentences.en: Zhang Wei also reminded that it took over two years between GPT-3 and GPT-3.5, while the robot's brain is a system, not determined by a single model, which is his different thinking from many others.
sentences.zh: 张伟还提醒，从GPT-3到GPT-3.5，历时两年多，而机器人的大脑是一个系统，不是由单一模型决定的，这是他与很多人不同的思维。

blocks.html_en: On skill learning, Zhang Wei also put forward a counter-conventional view: Skills don't need to be very general to be considered having a brain. A person who cannot tighten a screw or drive a car is still a normal person with a brain. The generality of skills does not represent the level of intelligence. Skill construction depends on what you want to learn first, then what next, and the corresponding data cost. Different skills have great differences in difficulty. For example, dancing, which does not require real-time interaction with the environment, is relatively simple, while climbing stairs, which requires real-time interaction, is much more difficult. Ollie, Zhujidi Dynamics' robot, climbing stairs is an example of real-time big brain and small brain fusion technology because stairs can be largely completed in a simulation environment; cleaning a table must rely on real machine data. Zhang Wei introduced that the company's COSA system, researched last year and released this year, can receive human commands, plan tasks through System 2, and then schedule navigation positioning and perform specific tasks, such as sending a package, where the robot would first take water to the customer and then deliver the package, generating actions in real time. Recently, COSA completed an important upgrade - a fully autonomous, real-time reasoning long-range task, without remote operation or a remote controller, completing full-body movement and operation in a home environment with a single model and a single skill, and the entire model is purely data-driven without expert rules. He stated that besides Figure, very few companies can complete such a general-purpose long-range mobile operation task, and systems that combine full-body movement and operation are particularly rare.

sentences.en: On skill learning, Zhang Wei also put forward a counter-conventional view: Skills don't need to be very general to be considered having a brain.
sentences.zh: 关于技能学习，张伟还提出了一个反传统的观点：技能不需要很通用才算有脑子。

sentences.en: A person who cannot tighten a screw or drive a car is still a normal person with a brain.
sentences.zh: 一个不会拧螺丝、不会开车的人，仍然是一个有大脑的正常人。

sentences.en: The generality of skills does not represent the level of intelligence.
sentences.zh: 技能的普遍性并不代表智力的高低。

sentences.en: Skill construction depends on what you want to learn first, then what next, and the corresponding data cost.
sentences.zh: 技能建设取决于你首先想学什么，然后再学什么，以及相应的数据成本。

sentences.en: Different skills have great differences in difficulty.
sentences.zh: 不同的技能难度差异很大。

sentences.en: For example, dancing, which does not require real-time interaction with the environment, is relatively simple, while climbing stairs, which requires real-time interaction, is much more difficult.
sentences.zh: 例如跳舞，不需要与环境实时交互，相对简单，而爬楼梯，需要实时交互，就困难得多。

sentences.en: Ollie, Zhujidi Dynamics' robot, climbing stairs is an example of real-time big brain and small brain fusion technology because stairs can be largely completed in a simulation environment; cleaning a table must rely on real machine data.
sentences.zh: 朱吉地动力的机器人Ollie，爬楼梯是实时大脑和小脑融合技术的一个例子，因为爬楼梯基本上可以在模拟环境中完成；清理表必须依赖真实的机器数据。

sentences.en: Zhang Wei introduced that the company's COSA system, researched last year and released this year, can receive human commands, plan tasks through System 2, and then schedule navigation positioning and perform specific tasks, such as sending a package, where the robot would first take water to the customer and then deliver the package, generating actions in real time.
sentences.zh: 张伟介绍，该公司去年研发、今年发布的COSA系统，可以接收人类指令，通过System 2规划任务，然后安排导航定位并执行特定任务，比如寄包裹，机器人先给顾客取水，然后再递送包裹，实时生成动作。

sentences.en: Recently, COSA completed an important upgrade - a fully autonomous, real-time reasoning long-range task, without remote operation or a remote controller, completing full-body movement and operation in a home environment with a single model and a single skill, and the entire model is purely data-driven without expert rules.
sentences.zh: 近日，COSA完成了一项重要升级——完全自主、实时推理的远程任务，无需远程操作或遥控器，以单一模型、单一技能在家庭环境下完成全身运动和操作，并且整个模型纯粹由数据驱动，无需专家规则。

sentences.en: He stated that besides Figure, very few companies can complete such a general-purpose long-range mobile operation task, and systems that combine full-body movement and operation are particularly rare.
sentences.zh: 他表示，除了Figure之外，很少有公司能够完成如此通用的远程移动操作任务，而全身运动与操作相结合的系统更是少之又少。

blocks.html_en: Regarding commercialization, Zhang Wei summarized the fundamental logic of humanoid robots as a general body plus apps. A single skill like dancing may have limited value, but by continuously adding different skills and applications without changing the robot's configuration, eventually a "absorbing" tipping point will appear, and enough applications will begin to gather around the general body. He believes that the host factory is the most critical in the industrial chain because it connects the upper and lower parts. Zhujidi Dynamics positions itself as a product-oriented host factory with brain capabilities, technically matching Figure comprehensively, and more aggressive in industrialization, with the slogan "serve people, not process," meaning that humanoid robots with two legs should serve people instead of production processes, so they do not prioritize industrial scenarios but are more suitable for reception and public services in human environments. He concluded that the key to commercialization is: the data cost of building a skill must be less than the value created by the skill, only then will the skill be worth doing.

sentences.en: Regarding commercialization, Zhang Wei summarized the fundamental logic of humanoid robots as a general body plus apps.
sentences.zh: 关于商业化，张伟将人形机器人的基本逻辑总结为通用身体+应用程序。

sentences.en: A single skill like dancing may have limited value, but by continuously adding different skills and applications without changing the robot's configuration, eventually a "absorbing" tipping point will appear, and enough applications will begin to gather around the general body.
sentences.zh: 像跳舞这样的单一技能可能价值有限，但通过在不改变机器人配置的情况下不断添加不同的技能和应用，最终会出现一个“吸收”的临界点，足够的应用将开始聚集在总体身体周围。

sentences.en: He believes that the host factory is the most critical in the industrial chain because it connects the upper and lower parts.
sentences.zh: 他认为主机厂是产业链中最关键的，因为它连接着上下游。

sentences.en: Zhujidi Dynamics positions itself as a product-oriented host factory with brain capabilities, technically matching Figure comprehensively, and more aggressive in industrialization, with the slogan "serve people, not process," meaning that humanoid robots with two legs should serve people instead of production processes, so they do not prioritize industrial scenarios but are more suitable for reception and public services in human environments.
sentences.zh: 珠鸡迪动力将自己定位为产品型、有大脑能力的主机厂，技术上全面匹配Figure，在工业化上更加激进，其口号是“为人服务，而不是过程”，意思是两条腿的仿人机器人应该为人服务，而不是为生产过程服务，因此不以工业场景为主，更适合人性化环境中的接待和公共服务。

sentences.en: He concluded that the key to commercialization is: the data cost of building a skill must be less than the value created by the skill, only then will the skill be worth doing.
sentences.zh: 他总结说，商业化的关键是：构建一项技能的数据成本必须小于该技能创造的价值，只有这样，该技能才值得做。

content_html_zh:
人形机器人什么时候才能最终走出展厅，成为解决现实问题的工具呢？ 这是2026世界人工智能大会上最常被问到的问题。 7月19日，朱基迪动力创始人、南方科技大学终身教授张伟上台，提出了一组颇为标新立异的判断：以人形机器人为代表的具身智能目前正处于指数​​级增长曲线，整个机器人的大脑系统已经达到了GPT-3的阶段。

珠鸡迪动力成立于2022年，张巍明确了公司的定位：以产品为导向、有大脑能力的主机厂。 近年来，公司的融资节奏已经清晰勾勒出资本对其路径的认可。 2023年10月，完成近2亿元天使轮及Pre-A轮融资，投资方包括锋锐资本、绿洲资本、联想创投、明石创投； 2024年7月，阿里巴巴领投A轮融资，这也是阿里巴巴在实体智能领域的首笔投资；

2025年，筑基动力在半年内完成了一系列5亿元A轮融资，获得了阿里巴巴、蔚来资本等顶级机构的支持，随后又获得了京东的战略投资。 双方计划在零售、物流、服务场景进行合作； 2026年1月，公司完成2亿美元B轮融资，引入阿联酋雷石资本、东方富海、基业资本，现有股东继续投资；就在今年7月，筑基动力完成近2亿美元Pre-IPO融资，投资方包括雷石资本、意大利财团、上市公司Lantek、IDG资本、合肥滨湖产业发展集团等。 张伟表示，公司已形成覆盖战略行业参与者、境内外金融投资机构的全面股东背景，产业化、国际化、市场化能力进一步增强。

张伟表示，很多人看到机器人仍然笨拙，做不了多少事情，所以倾向于以线性方式预测自己的未来，但行业正在经历指数级转型，不能等到结果才做出反应，否则就太晚了。 他用一个例子来说明线性推理的失败：比尔·盖茨花费了数十年时间并投入巨资来解决语言交互问题但从未成功。 如果问他在大模型出现之前什么时候能解决，他可能会说再过十年、二十年，但技术范式一旦改变，半年时间很多问题就解决了。

一个可能让很多人感到惊讶的观点是，张伟认为机器人很容易制造，人形机器人也很容易制造。 它们比光刻机、飞机甚至汽车更容易制造，零件数量约为汽车的十分之一。 那么为什么飞机天天在飞，汽车到处都是，而人形机器人却大多还在展厅里呢？ 他的回答是，并不是缺少能够制造身体的人，而是缺了本次大会主题所指出的核心能力——人工智能，尤其是源自物理世界数据的物理人工智能。 物理AI的特点是其数据不是来自互联网，而是来自现实生活中的物理体验，这使其成为AI最具挑战性的应用之一。 它的最终形式是物理人工智能，本质上是在模仿人脑。 张伟补充道，从概念的角度来看，人脑看起来并不是很难。 它的任务只是接收指令、理解意图、观察环境、计算如何移动，本质上是一个映射。 阻碍该行业的真正问题是缺乏数据。 他认为技术范式已经相对收敛到纯粹的数据驱动，剩下的更多是工程和细节问题。 所有探索本质上都是为了减少完成任务所需的数据成本。

对于大脑系统，张伟勾勒出一个三层结构，与Figure的架构类似，但细节上有显着差异。 最上层类似于人类的前额皮质，是由大语言模型、视觉语言模型甚至未来世界模型驱动的思维引擎，属于代理系统，负责记忆管理、指令收集、信息理解、任务规划和决策，只有思维，他称之为系统2。 中间层是视觉运动皮层，接收上层决策，观察环境，制定运动计划。 最底层是小脑和运动控制系统，负责完成动作，无需思考即可移动。 张伟强调，业界真正的挑战不是完善单层，而是在毫秒级实时系统中协调这三层，并与硬件共同优化。 珠鸡地动力将这一方向称为大脑和小脑技术的融合，这也是公司最重要的投资方向之一。

如果一定要用GPT时刻来比喻的话，张伟判断整个机器人的大脑系统已经达到了相当于GPT-3的早期阶段，这和很多人认为还很早期的判断完全不同。 但他警告说，机器人的大脑不是单一的模型，而是由大型语言模型、视觉语言模型、世界模型、内存管理、任务规划和运动控制组成的系统。 诸暨地动力的系统称为COSA。 模型代表能力，而大脑则是由多种模型加上记忆管理、情绪管理、任务规划等组成的一个整体系统。 他举了一个例子：如果系统2是由LLM驱动的，那么就像一个躺在床上的残疾人，可以思考但不能动；如果由VLM驱动，就像一个残疾人有眼睛可以看到世界；如果加上世界模型，就像史蒂芬·霍金学了物理，可以理解和预测物理世界。 具身智能旨在让这个智能系统2指导行动，比如帮助残疾人进行康复训练，通过数据和实践获得技能。 张伟还提醒，从GPT-3到GPT-3.5，历时两年多，而机器人的大脑是一个系统，不是由单一模型决定的，这是他与很多人不同的思维。

关于技能学习，张伟还提出了一个反传统的观点：技能不需要很通用才算有脑子。 一个不会拧螺丝、不会开车的人，仍然是一个有大脑的正常人。 技能的普遍性并不代表智力的高低。 技能建设取决于你首先想学什么，然后再学什么，以及相应的数据成本。 不同的技能难度差异很大。 例如跳舞，不需要与环境实时交互，相对简单，而爬楼梯，需要实时交互，就困难得多。 朱吉地动力的机器人Ollie，爬楼梯是实时大脑和小脑融合技术的一个例子，因为爬楼梯基本上可以在模拟环境中完成；清理表必须依赖真实的机器数据。 张伟介绍，该公司去年研发、今年发布的COSA系统，可以接收人类指令，通过System 2规划任务，然后安排导航定位并执行特定任务，比如寄包裹，机器人先给顾客取水，然后再递送包裹，实时生成动作。 近日，COSA完成了一项重要升级——完全自主、实时推理的远程任务，无需远程操作或遥控器，以单一模型、单一技能在家庭环境下完成全身运动和操作，并且整个模型纯粹由数据驱动，无需专家规则。 他表示，除了Figure之外，很少有公司能够完成如此通用的远程移动操作任务，而全身运动与操作相结合的系统更是少之又少。

关于商业化，张伟将人形机器人的基本逻辑总结为通用身体+应用程序。 像跳舞这样的单一技能可能价值有限，但通过在不改变机器人配置的情况下不断添加不同的技能和应用，最终会出现一个“吸收”的临界点，足够的应用将开始聚集在总体身体周围。 他认为主机厂是产业链中最关键的，因为它连接着上下游。 珠鸡迪动力将自己定位为产品型、有大脑能力的主机厂，技术上全面匹配Figure，在工业化上更加激进，其口号是“为人服务，而不是过程”，意思是两条腿的仿人机器人应该为人服务，而不是为生产过程服务，因此不以工业场景为主，更适合人性化环境中的接待和公共服务。 他总结说，商业化的关键是：构建一项技能的数据成本必须小于该技能创造的价值，只有这样，该技能才值得做。
